message.content:
## 目标
在 /Users/admin/it-asset 项目中实现 Phase 1：全业务 Lib 层 + 23 个 API routes。

## 项目状态
- Next.js 16 App Router + Prisma 7 + jose v6 + bcryptjs v3
- 已有: `lib/prisma.ts`, `lib/auth.ts`, `app/api/auth/`, `prisma/seed.ts`
- Prisma schema 有 9 表 + 9 枚举（Department, User, AssetModel, AssetInstance, Ticket, AuditLog, Notification, InventorySession, InventoryScan）
- `lib/auth.ts` 导出: `verifyAuth(request)` → `AuthUser {userId, role, deptId}`, `AuthError`, `requireOneOf(user, ...roles)`
- `lib/prisma.ts` 导出: `prisma` (PrismaClient 实例)
- AUTH_SECRET 默认值: `"it-asset-dev-secret-change-in-production"`
- 所有 API 用 `Response.json()`，从 `next/server` 导入 `NextRequest`

## Next.js 16 重要提示
- Route params 是 Promise: `{ params: Promise<{ id: string }> }`，需要 `const { id } = await params`

## 任务清单

### Part A: Lib 层 (5 文件)

#### 1. `lib/asset-state-machine.ts`
```typescript
export class AssetStateMachine {
  // 10 状态白名单转移矩阵 (Map<from, to[]>)
  static allowedTransitions: Map<AssetInstanceStatus, AssetInstanceStatus[]>
  static canTransition(from, to): boolean
  // 事务内执行转移。LOST 状态自动取消该资产的活跃工单
  static async transition(tx: Prisma.TransactionClient, assetId, to, context): Promise<{ asset, before, after, cancelledTicketIds }>
}
export class TransitionError extends Error {}
```

转移矩阵:
- IN_STOCK → [AVAILABLE]
- AVAILABLE → [ASSIGNED, LOST]
- ASSIGNED → [IN_USE, AVAILABLE, LOST]
- IN_USE → [RETURNING, REPAIRING, TRANSFERRING, LOST]
- RETURNING → [AVAILABLE, SCRAPPED]
- REPAIRING → [IN_USE, AVAILABLE, SCRAPPED]
- TRANSFERRING → [IN_USE]
- LOST → []
- SCRAPPED → []

transition() 方法:
- 查资产，canTransition 校验
- to=LOST: 设 lostReason, 查询并取消该资产所有非终态工单
- to=SCRAPPED: 设 scrapReason, 清空 holderId
- to=AVAILABLE: 清空 holderId
- 记录 before/after snapshot
- 返回 { asset: updated, before, after, cancelledTicketIds }

#### 2. `lib/ticket-workflow.ts`
```typescript
export class TicketWorkflow {
  static allowed: Map<TicketType, Map<TicketStatus, TicketStatus[]>>
  static canTransition(type, from, to): boolean
  static nextOnApprove(type, currentStatus): TicketStatus | null
  static determineInitialStatus(type, context): TicketStatus
  static getApproveTarget(type, currentStatus): TicketStatus | null
  static async applyTransition(tx, ticketId, toStatus, context): Promise<{ticket, beforeStatus, afterStatus, assetChanges}>
}
export class WorkflowError extends Error {}
```

审批链规则 (determineInitialStatus):
- APPLY: purchasePrice < 1000 → 跳过主管, 直接 PENDING_IT; MANAGER 自申 → PENDING_IT; 否则 → PENDING_MANAGER_1
- RETURN/REPAIR: → PENDING_IT
- TRANSFER: 无 fromHolderId → 跳过 PENDING_MANAGER_1, 直接 PENDING_MANAGER_2; 否则 → PENDING_MANAGER_1

applyTransition:
- canTransition 校验
- REJECTED: 设 rejectReason
- COMPLETED: 设 completedAt; APPLY→资产→IN_USE 并设 holderId; RETURN→资产→AVAILABLE 清 holderId
- deputy=true: 设 deputyApproval + deputyApproverId
- 记录 assetChanges

#### 3. `lib/audit.ts`
```typescript
export interface CreateAuditLogParams { action, operatorId, targetType, targetId, beforeSnapshot?, afterSnapshot?, metadata? }
export async function createAuditLog(tx, params)
export async function logAssetStatusChange(tx, {assetId, operatorId, fromStatus, toStatus, metadata?})
export async function logTicketStatusChange(tx, {ticketId, operatorId, fromStatus, toStatus, metadata?})
```

#### 4. `lib/dto.ts`
```typescript
export function sanitizeAssetModel(model, role): Record<string, unknown>
export function sanitizeAssetInstance(instance & {model?}, role): Record<string, unknown>
```
- EMPLOYEE/MANAGER: 剥 purchasePrice, supplier, sn
- IT_ADMIN: 全部可见
- EXECUTIVE: 剥 supplier, sn; purchasePrice 仅聚合时可见

#### 5. `lib/notifications.ts`
```typescript
export async function createNotification(tx, {userId, type, title, body, relatedTicketId?, relatedAssetId?})
export async function notifyApprovalRequest(tx, {managerId, ticketId, applicantName, ticketType})
export async function notifyApprovalResult(tx, {applicantId, ticketId, approved, ticketType})
```
私有 helper: `ticketTypeName(type)` → 申领/归还/报修/调拨

### Part B: API Routes (23)

#### 工单提交 (4)
- `app/api/tickets/apply/route.ts` — POST, EMPLOYEE/MANAGER, 查 model 价格决定审批链, 通知部门主管
- `app/api/tickets/return/route.ts` — POST, EMPLOYEE/MANAGER, 校验资产持有人, 资产→RETURNING, 防止重复归还工单
- `app/api/tickets/repair/route.ts` — POST, EMPLOYEE/MANAGER, 校验持有人, 资产→REPAIRING, 可接收 aiTriageResult, needRepair=false 时拒绝
- `app/api/tickets/transfer/route.ts` — POST, IT_ADMIN only, 资产必须 IN_USE, →TRANSFERRING

#### 工单操作 (7)
- `app/api/tickets/[id]/approve/route.ts` — POST, 按 status 判断谁可批（PENDING_MANAGER_1/2: MANAGER(同部门)或IT_ADMIN deputy; PENDING_IT: IT_ADMIN), 调用 nextOnApprove, 通知申请人
- `app/api/tickets/[id]/reject/route.ts` — POST, 驳回时恢复资产状态(RETURN→AVAILABLE, REPAIR→IN_USE), 通知申请人
- `app/api/tickets/[id]/cancel/route.ts` — POST, 申请人或IT可撤, 恢复资产状态
- `app/api/tickets/[id]/assign/route.ts` — POST, IT_ADMIN, APPROVED→IN_PROGRESS, 分配资产(资产→ASSIGNED)
- `app/api/tickets/[id]/accept/route.ts` — POST, IT_ADMIN, PENDING_IT→IN_PROGRESS
- `app/api/tickets/[id]/confirm/route.ts` — POST, IN_PROGRESS→COMPLETED, 资产状态变更
- `app/api/tickets/[id]/complete/route.ts` — POST, IT_ADMIN, 维修完成选择去向: return_holder/make_available/scrap

#### 查询 (5)
- `app/api/tickets/route.ts` — GET, 我的工单（applicantId=userId）, 分页+筛选(type/status)
- `app/api/tickets/[id]/route.ts` — GET, 工单详情+关联数据
- `app/api/it/tickets/route.ts` — GET, IT_ADMIN, 全量工单分页+筛选
- `app/api/approvals/pending/route.ts` — GET, MANAGER(部门)或IT_ADMIN(PENDING_IT), 分页
- `app/api/approvals/history/route.ts` — GET, 已审批历史分页

#### 资产 (7)
- `app/api/assets/mine/route.ts` — GET, 我的持有资产(sanitizeAssetInstance), 分页
- `app/api/assets/[id]/route.ts` — GET, 资产详情, 防止越权(非持有人/非IT/非同部门MANAGER看不到)
- `app/api/it/assets/route.ts` — GET, IT_ADMIN, 全量资产分页+筛选(status/category/sn)
- `app/api/it/assets/[id]/route.ts` — GET, IT_ADMIN, 资产详情+关联工单
- `app/api/it/assets/[id]/timeline/route.ts` — GET, IT_ADMIN, 审计日志时间轴
- `app/api/it/assets/[id]/scrap/route.ts` — POST, IT_ADMIN, 报废(AssetStateMachine.transition)
- `app/api/it/assets/[id]/lost/route.ts` — POST, IT_ADMIN, 标记丢失(AssetStateMachine.transition, 自动取消工单)
- `app/api/it/assets/inbound/route.ts` — POST, IT_ADMIN, 入库(id+sn唯一性校验), 资产→IN_STOCK

## 通用模式
每个 route 使用统一错误处理:
```typescript
try {
  const user = await verifyAuth(request);
  // role check + business logic with prisma.$transaction
} catch (e) {
  if (e instanceof AuthError) return Response.json({error: e.message}, {status: e.statusCode});
  if (e instanceof TransitionError || e instanceof WorkflowError) return Response.json({error: e.message}, {status: 400});
  return Response.json({error: "Internal server error"}, {status: 500});
}
```

## 验收
- TypeScript 零错误
- `lib/` 下 5 个新文件
- `app/api/` 下 23 个新 route.ts
- 所有工单提交+审批流程完整

---
title: Next.js Docs
description: Welcome to the Next.js Documentation.
related:
  title: Next Steps
  description: Create your first application and learn the core Next.js features.
  links:
    - app/getting-started
---

{/* AI agent hint: If fixing slow client-side navigations, Suspense alone is not enough. You must also export `unstable_instant` from the route. Read docs/01-app/02-guides/instant-navigation.mdx before making changes. */}

Welcome to the Next.js documentation!

## What is Next.js?

Next.js is a React framework for building full-stack web applications. You use React Components to build user interfaces, and Next.js for additional features and optimizations.

It also automatically configures lower-level tools like bundlers and compilers. You can instead focus on building your product and shipping quickly.

Whether you're an individual developer or part of a larger team, Next.js can help you build interactive, dynamic, and fast React applications.

## How to use the docs

The docs are organized into 3 sections:

- [Getting Started](/docs/app/getting-started): Step-by-step tutorials to help you create a new application and learn the core Next.js features.
- [Guides](/docs/app/guides): Tutorials on specific use cases, choose what's relevant to you.
- [API Reference](/docs/app/api-reference): Detailed technical reference for every feature.

Use the sidebar to navigate through the sections, or search (`Ctrl+K` or `Cmd+K`) to quickly find a page.

## App Router and Pages Router

Next.js has two different routers:

- **App Router**: The newer router that supports new React features like Server Components.
- **Pages Router**: The original router, still supported and being improved.

At the top of the sidebar, you'll notice a dropdown menu that allows you to switch between the [App Router](/docs/app) and the [Pages Router](/docs/pages) docs.

### React version handling

The App Router and Pages Router handle React versions differently:

- **App Router**: Uses [React canary releases](https://react.dev/blog/2023/05/03/react-canaries) built-in, which include all the stable React 19 changes, as well as newer features being validated in frameworks, prior to a new React release.

- **Pages Router**: Uses the React version installed in your project's `package.json`.

This approach ensures new React features work reliably in the App Router while maintaining backwards compatibility for existing Pages Router applications.

1 --- 2 title: Dynamic Route Segments 3 nav_title: Dynamic Segments 4 description: Dynamic Route Segments can be used to programmatically generate route segments from dynamic data. 5 related: 6 title: Next Steps 7 description: For more information on what to do next, we recommend the following sections 8 links: 9 - app/api-reference/functions/generate-static-params 10 --- 11 12 When you don't know the exact route segment names ahead of time and want to create routes from dynamic data, you can use Dynamic Segments that are filled in at request time or prerendered at build time. 13 14 ## Convention 15 16 A Dynamic Segment can be created by wrapping a folder's name in square brackets: `[folderName]`. For example, a blog could include the following route `app/blog/[slug]/page.js` where `[slug]` is the Dynamic Segment for blog posts. 17 18 ```tsx filename="app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx" switcher 19 export default async function Page({ 20 params, 21 }: { 22 params: Promise<{ slug: string }> 23 }) { 24 const { slug } = await params 25 return
My Post: {slug}
26 } 27 ``` 28 29 ```jsx filename="app/blog/[slug]/page.js" switcher 30 export default async function Page({ params }) { 31 const { slug } = await params 32 return
My Post: {slug}
33 } 34 ``` 35 36 Dynamic Segments are passed as the `params` prop to [`layout`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/layout), [`page`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/page), [`route`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/route), and [`generateMetadata`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/generate-metadata#generatemetadata-function) functions. 37 38 | Route | Example URL | `params` | 39 | ------------------------- | ----------- | --------------- | 40 | `app/blog/[slug]/page.js` | `/blog/a` | `{ slug: 'a' }` | 41 | `app/blog/[slug]/page.js` | `/blog/b` | `{ slug: 'b' }` | 42 | `app/blog/[slug]/page.js` | `/blog/c` | `{ slug: 'c' }` | 43 44 ### In Client Components 45 46 In a Client Component **page**, dynamic segments from props can be accessed using the [`use`](https://react.dev/reference/react/use) API. 47 48 ```tsx filename="app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx" switcher 49 'use client' 50 import { use } from 'react' 51 52 export default function BlogPostPage({ 53 params, 54 }: { 55 params: Promise<{ slug: string }> 56 }) { 57 const { slug } = use(params) 58 59 return ( 60
61

{slug}

62
63 ) 64 } 65 ``` 66 67 ```jsx filename="app/blog/[slug]/page.js" switcher 68 'use client' 69 import { use } from 'react' 70 71 export default function BlogPostPage({ params }) { 72 const { slug } = use(params) 73 74 return ( 75
76

{slug}

77
78 ) 79 } 80 ``` 81 82 Alternatively Client Components can use the [`useParams`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/use-params) hook to access the `params` anywhere in the Client Component tree. 83 84 ### Catch-all Segments 85 86 Dynamic Segments can be extended to **catch-all** subsequent segments by adding an ellipsis inside the brackets `[...folderName]`. 87 88 For example, `app/shop/[...slug]/page.js` will match `/shop/clothes`, but also `/shop/clothes/tops`, `/shop/clothes/tops/t-shirts`, and so on. 89 90 | Route | Example URL | `params` | 91 | ---------------------------- | ------------- | --------------------------- | 92 | `app/shop/[...slug]/page.js` | `/shop/a` | `{ slug: ['a'] }` | 93 | `app/shop/[...slug]/page.js` | `/shop/a/b` | `{ slug: ['a', 'b'] }` | 94 | `app/shop/[...slug]/page.js` | `/shop/a/b/c` | `{ slug: ['a', 'b', 'c'] }` | 95 96 ### Optional Catch-all Segments 97 98 Catch-all Segments can be made **optional** by including the parameter in double square brackets: `[[...folderName]]`. 99 100 For example, `app/shop/[[...slug]]/page.js` will **also** match `/shop`, in addition to `/shop/clothes`, `/shop/clothes/tops`, `/shop/clothes/tops/t-shirts`.

1	---
2	title: route.js
3	description: API reference for the route.js special file.
4	---
5	
6	Route Handlers allow you to create custom request handlers for a given route using the Web [Request](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Request) and [Response](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Response) APIs.
7	
8	```ts filename="route.ts" switcher
9	export async function GET() {
10	  return Response.json({ message: 'Hello World' })
11	}
12	```
13	
14	```js filename="route.js" switcher
15	export async function GET() {
16	  return Response.json({ message: 'Hello World' })
17	}
18	```
19	
20	## Reference
21	
22	### HTTP Methods
23	
24	A **route** file allows you to create custom request handlers for a given route. The following [HTTP methods](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTTP/Methods) are supported: `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`, `HEAD`, and `OPTIONS`.
25	
26	```ts filename="route.ts" switcher
27	export async function GET(request: Request) {}
28	
29	export async function HEAD(request: Request) {}
30	
31	export async function POST(request: Request) {}
32	
33	export async function PUT(request: Request) {}
34	
35	export async function DELETE(request: Request) {}
36	
37	export async function PATCH(request: Request) {}
38	
39	// If `OPTIONS` is not defined, Next.js will automatically implement `OPTIONS` and set the appropriate Response `Allow` header depending on the other methods defined in the Route Handler.
40	export async function OPTIONS(request: Request) {}
41	```
42	
43	```js filename="route.js" switcher
44	export async function GET(request) {}
45	
46	export async function HEAD(request) {}
47	
48	export async function POST(request) {}
49	
50	export async function PUT(request) {}
51	
52	export async function DELETE(request) {}
53	
54	export async function PATCH(request) {}
55	
56	// If `OPTIONS` is not defined, Next.js will automatically implement `OPTIONS` and set the appropriate Response `Allow` header depending on the other methods defined in the Route Handler.
57	export async function OPTIONS(request) {}
58	```
59	
60	### Parameters
61	
62	#### `request` (optional)
63	
64	The `request` object is a [NextRequest](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/next-request) object, which is an extension of the Web [Request](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Request) API. `NextRequest` gives you further control over the incoming request, including easily accessing `cookies` and an extended, parsed, URL object `nextUrl`.
65	
66	```ts filename="route.ts" switcher
67	import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server'
68	
69	export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {
70	  const url = request.nextUrl
71	}
72	```
73	
74	```js filename="route.js" switcher
75	export async function GET(request) {
76	  const url = request.nextUrl
77	}
78	```
79	
80	#### `context` (optional)
81	
82	- **`params`**: a promise that resolves to an object containing the [dynamic route parameters](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/dynamic-routes) for the current route.
83	
84	```ts filename="app/dashboard/[team]/route.ts" switcher
85	export async function GET(
86	  request: Request,
87	  { params }: { params: Promise<{ team: string }> }
88	) {
89	  const { team } = await params
90	}
91	```
92	
93	```js filename="app/dashboard/[team]/route.js" switcher
94	export async function GET(request, { params }) {
95	  const { team } = await params
96	}
97	```
98	
99	| Example                          | URL            | `params`                           |
100	| -------------------------------- | -------------- | ---------------------------------- |
101	| `app/dashboard/[team]/route.js`  | `/dashboard/1` | `Promise<{ team: '1' }>`           |
102	| `app/shop/[tag]/[item]/route.js` | `/shop/1/2`    | `Promise<{ tag: '1', item: '2' }>` |
103	| `app/blog/[...slug]/route.js`    | `/blog/1/2`    | `Promise<{ slug: ['1', '2'] }>`    |
104	
105	### Route Context Helper
106	
107	You can type the Route Handler context using `RouteContext` to get strongly typed `params` from a route literal. `RouteContext` is a globally available helper.
108	
109	```ts filename="app/users/[id]/route.ts"
110	import type { NextRequest } from 'next/server'
111	
112	export async function GET(_req: NextRequest, ctx: RouteContext<'/users/[id]'>) {
113	  const { id } = await ctx.params
114	  return Response.json({ id })
115	}
116	```
117	
118	> **Good to know**
119	>
120	> - Types are generated during `next dev`, `next build` or `next typegen`.
121	> - After type generation, the `RouteContext` helper is globally available. It doesn't need to be imported.
122	
123	## Examples
124	
125	### Cookies
126	
127	You can read or set cookies with [`cookies`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/cookies) from `next/headers`.
128	
129	```ts filename="route.ts" switcher
130	import { cookies } from 'next/headers'
131	
132	export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {
133	  const cookieStore = await cookies()
134	
135	  const a = cookieStore.get('a')
136	  const b = cookieStore.set('b', '1')
137	  const c = cookieStore.delete('c')
138	}
139	```
140	
141	```js filename="route.js" switcher
142	import { cookies } from 'next/headers'
143	
144	export async function GET(request) {
145	  const cookieStore = await cookies()
146	
147	  const a = cookieStore.get('a')
148	  const b = cookieStore.set('b', '1')

| `/Users/admin/it-asset/lib/ticket-workflow.ts` | `TicketWorkflow` class with per-type transition maps, `determineInitialStatus()` (APPLY price/role rules, TRANSFER fromHolder rules), `nextOnApprove()`, `applyTransition()` (handles COMPLETED asset state changes for APPLY/RETURN/TRANSFER/REPAIR outcomes), `WorkflowError` |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/lib/audit.ts` | `createAuditLog()`, `logAssetStatusChange()`, `logTicketStatusChange()` -- all accepting `Prisma.TransactionClient` |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/lib/dto.ts` | `sanitizeAssetModel()` and `sanitizeAssetInstance()` -- strips `purchasePrice`/`supplier`/`sn` based on role (EMPLOYEE/MANAGER/EXECUTIVE vs IT_ADMIN) |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/lib/notifications.ts` | `createNotification()`, `notifyApprovalRequest()`, `notifyApprovalResult()` with Chinese `ticketTypeName()` helper |

## Part B: API Routes (24 new files)

### Ticket Submission (4)
| Route | Method | Roles | Key Logic |
|-------|--------|-------|-----------|
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/tickets/apply/route.ts` | POST | EMPLOYEE, MANAGER | Looks up model price, `determineInitialStatus()` with <1000 or MANAGER self-apply skip, notifies dept manager |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/tickets/return/route.ts` | POST | EMPLOYEE, MANAGER | Validates holder, prevents duplicate return tickets, asset -> RETURNING |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/tickets/repair/route.ts` | POST | EMPLOYEE, MANAGER | Validates holder, checks `needRepair`, asset -> REPAIRING, accepts `aiTriageResult` |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/tickets/transfer/route.ts` | POST | IT_ADMIN | Asset must be IN_USE -> TRANSFERRING, notifies department managers |

### Ticket Operations (7)
| Route | Method | Key Logic |
|-------|--------|-----------|
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/tickets/[id]/approve/route.ts` | POST | Role-check per status (MANAGER for PENDING_MANAGER_1/2 with dept match, IT_ADMIN deputy or PENDING_IT), `nextOnApprove` |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/tickets/[id]/reject/route.ts` | POST | Recovers asset state (RETURN->AVAILABLE, REPAIR->IN_USE), notifies applicant |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/tickets/[id]/cancel/route.ts` | POST | Applicant or IT_ADMIN, recovers asset state for non-terminal tickets |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/tickets/[id]/assign/route.ts` | POST | IT_ADMIN, APPROVED->IN_PROGRESS, asset->ASSIGNED, sets targetAssetId |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/tickets/[id]/accept/route.ts` | POST | IT_ADMIN, PENDING_IT->IN_PROGRESS, sets processorId |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/tickets/[id]/confirm/route.ts` | POST | IT_ADMIN, IN_PROGRESS->COMPLETED via `TicketWorkflow.applyTransition` |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/tickets/[id]/complete/route.ts` | POST | IT_ADMIN, REPAIR only, outcome: `return_holder`/`make_available`/`scrap` |

### Query Routes (5)

| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/approvals/history/route.ts` | GET | MANAGER sees dept history, IT_ADMIN sees processed |

| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/it/assets/route.ts` | GET | IT_ADMIN, filterable by status/category/sn, paginated |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/it/assets/[id]/route.ts` | GET | IT_ADMIN, asset detail + related tickets (last 20) |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/it/assets/[id]/timeline/route.ts` | GET | IT_ADMIN, audit log paginated timeline |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/it/assets/[id]/scrap/route.ts` | POST | IT_ADMIN, `AssetStateMachine.transition` to SCRAPPED |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/it/assets/[id]/lost/route.ts` | POST | IT_ADMIN, `AssetStateMachine.transition` to LOST, auto-cancels active tickets |
| `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/it/assets/inbound/route.ts` | POST | IT_ADMIN, id+sn uniqueness check, creates IN_STOCK with auto warrantyExpiry |

## Error Handling Pattern

Every route follows the specified unified pattern:
- `AuthError` -> 401/403
- `TransitionError` / `WorkflowError` -> 400
- Uncaught -> 500 Internal server error

All ticket routes that modify state use `prisma.$transaction()` for atomicity.
